summary: Official policy-backed PowerShell execution policy setting. The app writes RemoteSigned under the documented policy path, which allows local scripts while requiring remote scripts to be signed.

setting.name: PowerShell Script Execution Policy
setting.casual_explanation: PowerShell execution policy controls which scripts are allowed to run. RemoteSigned allows local scripts and requires downloaded scripts to be signed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want local scripts to run more easily on development machines without opening the door as wide as Unrestricted.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: RemoteSigned is more permissive than a locked-down default, but still safer than allowing every script without checks.
setting.professional_notes: PowerShellExecutionPolicy.admx maps the execution policy under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ExecutionPolicy. The local ADMX file includes RemoteSigned as an allowed policy value, and the local ADML text describes it as allowing local scripts and remote signed scripts. The app writes RemoteSigned on that documented policy path.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows PowerShell keeps its normal effective execution-policy behavior because no machine policy override is set here.

allowed_values.label: Allow local scripts and remote signed scripts
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell allows local scripts and requires remote scripts to be signed.

allowed_values.label: Only signed scripts
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell requires all scripts to be signed.

allowed_values.label: All scripts allowed
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell allows scripts without the RemoteSigned restriction.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes ExecutionPolicy = RemoteSigned on the documented policy path.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems where the PowerShell execution-policy machine policy is not configured
windows_defaults.notes: This record tracks the Group Policy path only, not every possible effective execution-policy scope.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Machines that should not force a developer-oriented PowerShell policy
avoid_for: Developer machines that need easier local script execution
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to avoid forcing a machine-wide PowerShell script policy.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a machine-wide developer-friendly script policy.
tradeoffs: PowerShell keeps the default policy resolution behavior.
No machine-wide developer execution policy is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: Developer RemoteSigned

states.rationale: Use the documented machine policy value that allows local scripts and remote signed scripts.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want local scripts to run more easily while keeping a signature check for downloaded scripts.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want stricter script execution controls than RemoteSigned.
tradeoffs: Local development scripts become easier to run.
The system is more permissive than a more locked-down script policy.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft PowerShellExecutionPolicy.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps ExecutionPolicy under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell and includes RemoteSigned as one of the allowed values.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft PowerShellExecutionPolicy.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text labels RemoteSigned as allowing local scripts and remote signed scripts.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes ExecutionPolicy = RemoteSigned on the documented PowerShell policy path.

decision.why: The local Microsoft policy files clearly document the path and allowed values, and the app writes the documented RemoteSigned value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="EnableScripts" class="Both" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell" valueName="EnableScripts"> <enum id="ExecutionPolicy" valueName="ExecutionPolicy" required="true">; PowerShellExecutionPolicy.adml: "Turn on Script Execution" and "Allow local scripts and remote signed scripts".
validation_proof.notes: Local official Microsoft ADMX/ADML files document the PowerShell policy path and the ExecutionPolicy enum, including RemoteSigned. The app writes ExecutionPolicy = RemoteSigned on the documented policy path, so this record is re-validated under schema 1.1.
